response: This code implements the consistent hashing distributed system pattern, which assigns data items to cluster nodes to minimize data rebalancing overhead when nodes are added or removed. The implementation creates 100 virtual replica nodes per physical node to evenly spread load across the sorted hash ring, and the returned get_node function uses binary search (via `bisect`) to locate the responsible node for any input item, wrapping around the ring with modulo to handle items that hash past the largest ring position.
